---
title: "02_final_version"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-24"
---
Batch correction of bulk raw counts using ComBat-Seq:
 - Loads raw count matrix (gene-level)
 - Builds sample metada (batch, treatment, diagnosis) from sample names and merge
 - PCA pre/post (logCPM) for QC
 - Applies ComBat-Seq preserving biological structure
 - Saves corrected/CPM-corrected/logCPM-corrected matrices + PCA and Heatmaps plots


## 0. Configuration directories and libraries

Include all necessary libraries and paths to the corresponding files

```{r setup}

library(sva)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)

in_dir   <- "/home/jose/Desktop/RNAseq/results/counts/bulk/"
diagnosis_dir <- "/home/jose/Desktop/RNAseq/data/Diagnosis_data"
out_dir  <- "/home/jose/Desktop/RNAseq/results/counts/bulk/"
plot_dir <- "/home/jose/Desktop/RNAseq/results/plots/combatseq"
dir.create(plot_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Acces to data

in_counts   <- file.path(in_dir, "rawcounts.csv")
in_diagnosis <- file.path(diagnosis_dir, "Mostres_RNAseqJose.csv")

```

## 1. Load counts

Generate the dataframe to extract the information

```{r counts}

rawcounts_df <- read.csv(in_counts, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
colnames(rawcounts_df)[1] <- "Gene"
rawcounts_df[, -1] <- lapply(rawcounts_df[, -1, drop = FALSE], as.numeric)

rawcounts <- as.matrix(rawcounts_df[, -1, drop = FALSE])
rownames(rawcounts) <- rawcounts_df$Gene

# Checks

stopifnot(is.matrix(rawcounts))
stopifnot(nrow(rawcounts) > 0, ncol(rawcounts) > 0)
stopifnot(all(colSums(rawcounts) > 0))

message("Counts loaded: ", nrow(rawcounts), " genes x ", ncol(rawcounts), " samples")

```

## 2. Build coldata

Generate subgroups to consider the batch and treatment

```{r coldata}

sample_names <- colnames(rawcounts)

# Extract Batch (e.g. FC1. FC2, FC3) and treatment (e.g. Basal, LPS)

batch <- ifelse(grepl("_FC1_", sample_names, ignore.case = TRUE), "FC1",
                ifelse(grepl("_FC2_", sample_names, ignore.case = TRUE), "FC2", "FC3"))

if (all(batch == "FC3")) {
  warning("All batches assigned to FC3. Check '_FC1_', '_FC2_' patterns in sample names.")
  message("Sample name examples: ", paste(head(sample_names, 3), collapse = " | "))
}
message("Batch distribution:"); print(table(batch))

treatment <- ifelse(grepl("basal", sample_names, ignore.case = TRUE), "Basal", "LPS")
message("Treatment distribution:"); print(table(treatment))

# Extract donor ID for posterior merge with Diagnosis (assumes pattern SXXX_NNN at start)

donor_id <- sub("^[^_]+_[^_]+_([^_]+)_.*", "\\1", sample_names)

coldata <- data.frame(
  sample    = sample_names,
  treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("Basal", "LPS")),
  batch     = factor(batch),
  donor_id  = factor(donor_id),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
rownames(coldata) <- sample_names

```

## 3. Merge diagnosis data

Extract data from the diagnosis file and match it with the general dataframe 

```{r diagnosis}
meta_df <- read.csv(in_diagnosis, header=TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)

# Consider the titles of columns to extract the data

meta_id_col   <- "Sample_ID"   # column with partial sample ID (e.g. "CRR01")
meta_diag_col <- "Diagnosis"    # column with diagnosis label (e.g. "TEA", "TOC", "Tourette")

# Match information of both side

coldata$diagnosis <- meta_df[[meta_diag_col]][match(coldata$donor_id, meta_df[[meta_id_col]])]

# Keep only first diagnostic in combined labels (e.g."TOC+TEA" -> "TOC")

coldata$diagnosis <- sub("\\+.*", "", coldata$diagnosis)

# Check that all samples did match to a diagnosis

if (any(is.na(coldata$diagnosis))) {
  message("Some diagnoses did not match directly. Trying partial match...")
  for (i in which(is.na(coldata$diagnosis))) {hit <- which(sapply(meta_df[[meta_id_col]],function(id) grepl(id, coldata$donor_id[i], fixed = TRUE))) 
if (length(hit) == 1) coldata$diagnosis[i] <- meta_df[[meta_diag_col]][hit]}}

n_na_diag <- sum(is.na(coldata$diagnosis))

if (n_na_diag > 0) {
  warning(n_na_diag, " samples without diagnosis. Check 'meta_id_col'.")
  print(coldata[is.na(coldata$diagnosis), c("sample", "donor_id")])} else {message("Diagnosis assigned to all samples successfully.")}

coldata$diagnosis <- factor(coldata$diagnosis)

message("Diagnosis distribution:"); print(table(coldata$diagnosis))
```

## 4. Processing/Normalizing for PCA (logCPM)

Normalization by logCPM and Heatmap generation

```{r}
# logCPM + PCA

calc_logcpm_pca <- function(count_matrix, coldata, scale_pca = TRUE) {

  lib_sizes <- colSums(count_matrix)
  cpm <- t(t(count_matrix) / lib_sizes * 1e6)
  logcpm <- log2(cpm + 1)
  
  # Select 500 genes and variables for a clean PCA
  var_genes <- apply(logcpm, 1, var)
  select_genes <- names(sort(var_genes, decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(500, length(var_genes))]
  logcpm_top <- logcpm[select_genes, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # PCA (Use scale = TRUE making a standardization of each gene to emphasize in relative expression patterns across samples instead in only genes with large absolute variance)
  pca <- prcomp(t(logcpm_top), scale. = scale_pca)
  percentVar <- round(100 * (pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2)), 1)
  
  pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca$x[, 1:2])
  pca_df <- cbind(pca_df, coldata[rownames(pca_df), ])
  
  list(pca_df = pca_df, percentVar = percentVar, logcpm = logcpm)
}

# Heatmap 

make_heatmap <- function(logcpm_matrix, annotation_vars, title,
                         out_file, top_n = 500) {
  
  # Select top N most variable genes
  var_genes  <- apply(logcpm_matrix, 1, var)
  top_genes  <- names(sort(var_genes, decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(top_n, length(var_genes))]
  mat        <- logcpm_matrix[top_genes, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # Scale rows (z-score) for better visual contrast
  mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat)))
  
  # Build annotation dataframe
  ann_df <- coldata[colnames(mat), annotation_vars, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Build annotation colors
  ann_colors <- list()
  
  if ("treatment" %in% annotation_vars) {
    ann_colors$treatment <- c("Basal" = "#4e9af1", "LPS" = "#e05c5c")
  }
  
  if ("diagnosis" %in% annotation_vars) {
    diag_levels <- levels(coldata$diagnosis)
    diag_pal    <- brewer.pal(max(3, length(diag_levels)), "Set1")[seq_along(diag_levels)]
    names(diag_pal) <- diag_levels
    ann_colors$diagnosis <- diag_pal
  }
  
  if ("batch" %in% annotation_vars) {
    batch_levels <- levels(coldata$batch)
    batch_pal    <- brewer.pal(max(3, length(batch_levels)), "Dark2")[seq_along(batch_levels)]
    names(batch_pal) <- batch_levels
    ann_colors$batch <- batch_pal
  }
  
  # Draw and save heatmap
  png(out_file, width = 10, height = 9, units = "in", res = 300)
  pheatmap(
    mat_scaled,
    annotation_col  = ann_df,
    annotation_colors = ann_colors,
    cluster_rows    = TRUE,
    cluster_cols    = TRUE,
    show_rownames   = FALSE,
    show_colnames   = FALSE,
    fontsize         = 9,
    color            = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu")))(100),
    main             = title,
    border_color     = NA
  )
  dev.off()
  message("Heatmap saved: ", out_file)
}

```

## 5. PCA previous pre-correction via ComBat-Seq

```{r previous PCA}

pre <- calc_logcpm_pca(rawcounts, coldata)

# PCA Plot 1: Raw counts â€” treatment + batch ------------------------------
p1 <- ggplot(pre$pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                             color = treatment, shape = batch)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Basal" = "#4e9af1", "LPS" = "#e05c5c")) +
  labs(title    = "1. Plot â€” Raw Counts (Pre-ComBat)",
       subtitle = "Color = Treatment  |  Shape = Batch",
       x = paste0("PC1: ", pre$percentVar[1], "% variance"),
       y = paste0("PC2: ", pre$percentVar[2], "% variance")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "1.Plot_PCA_RawCounts.png"),
       p1, width = 7, height = 6, dpi = 300)
message("Plot 1 saved.")

# Heatmap 1: Raw counts â€” treatment only ----------------------------------
make_heatmap(
  logcpm_matrix   = pre$logcpm,
  annotation_vars = "treatment",
  title    = "1.Heatmap â€” Raw Counts | Top 500 variable genes (Treatment)",
  out_file = file.path(plot_dir, "1.Heatmap_Raw_Treatment.png")
)

```

## 6. Applicate ComBat- Seq (Correct technical problems such as Batch effect)

```{r ComBat-Seq}
combatseqcounts <- ComBat_seq(
  counts = rawcounts,
  batch = coldata$batch,
  group = coldata$treatment
)
```

## 7. PCA post-correction via ComBat-Seq

```{r post PCA}

post <- calc_logcpm_pca(combatseqcounts, coldata)

# PCA Plot 2: Post-ComBat â€” treatment + batch (batch QC) ------------------
p2 <- ggplot(post$pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                              color = treatment, shape = batch)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Basal" = "#4e9af1", "LPS" = "#e05c5c")) +
  labs(title    = "2. Plot â€” Post-ComBat-Seq (Batch corrected)",
       subtitle = "Color = Treatment  |  Shape = Batch",
       x = paste0("PC1: ", post$percentVar[1], "% variance"),
       y = paste0("PC2: ", post$percentVar[2], "% variance")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "2.Plot_PCA_PostComBat_BatchQC.png"),
       p2, width = 7, height = 6, dpi = 300)
message("Plot 2 saved.")

# PCA Plot 3: Post-ComBat â€” diagnosis + treatment (biological variance) ---
p3 <- ggplot(post$pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                              color = diagnosis, shape = treatment)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", na.value = "grey60") +
  labs(title    = "3. Plot â€” Post-ComBat-Seq (Biological variance)",
       subtitle = "Color = Diagnosis  |  Shape = Treatment",
       x = paste0("PC1: ", post$percentVar[1], "% variance"),
       y = paste0("PC2: ", post$percentVar[2], "% variance")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "3.Plot_PCA_PostComBat_Diagnosis.png"),
       p3, width = 7, height = 6, dpi = 300)
message("Plot 3 saved.")

# Heatmap 2: Post-ComBat â€” treatment only ---------------------------------
make_heatmap(
  logcpm_matrix   = post$logcpm,
  annotation_vars = "treatment",
  title    = "2.Heatmap â€” Post-ComBat-Seq | Top 500 variable genes (Treatment)",
  out_file = file.path(plot_dir, "2.Heatmap_PostComBat_Treatment.png")
)

## Heatmap 3: Post-ComBat â€” treatment + diagnosis --------------------------
make_heatmap(
  logcpm_matrix   = post$logcpm,
  annotation_vars = c("treatment", "diagnosis"),
  title    = "3.Heatmap â€” Post-ComBat-Seq | Top 500 variable genes (Treatment + Diagnosis)",
  out_file = file.path(plot_dir, "3.Heatmap_PostComBat_Treatment_Diagnosis.png")
)

```

## 8. Donor effect correction for visualization

By the use of removeBatchEffect data is corrected considering that donor effect whereas protecting the variance of interest

```{r donor effect}

library(limma)

logcpm_no_donor <- removeBatchEffect(
  post$logcpm,
  batch = coldata$donor_id,        # removes inter-individual baseline shifts
  design = model.matrix(~ treatment, data = coldata)  # preserves treatment effect
)

# PCA on donor-corrected logCPM
var_genes_d  <- apply(logcpm_no_donor, 1, var)
top_genes_d  <- names(sort(var_genes_d, decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(500, sum(var_genes_d > 0))]
pca_donor    <- prcomp(t(logcpm_no_donor[top_genes_d, ]), scale. = FALSE)
pctVar_donor <- round(100 * (pca_donor$sdev^2 / sum(pca_donor$sdev^2)), 1)

pca_df_donor <- as.data.frame(pca_donor$x[, 1:2])
pca_df_donor <- cbind(pca_df_donor, coldata[rownames(pca_df_donor), , drop = FALSE])

## Plot 4: Donor-corrected â€” treatment + batch -----------------------------
p4 <- ggplot(pca_df_donor, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                               color = treatment, shape = batch)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Basal" = "#4e9af1", "LPS" = "#e05c5c")) +
  labs(title    = "4. Plot â€” Donor-corrected (Visualization only)",
       subtitle = "Color = Treatment  |  Shape = Batch",
       x = paste0("PC1: ", pctVar_donor[1], "% variance"),
       y = paste0("PC2: ", pctVar_donor[2], "% variance")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.caption = element_text(color = "firebrick", size = 8))

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "4.Plot_PCA_DonorCorrected_Treatment.png"),
       p4, width = 7, height = 6, dpi = 300)
message("Plot 4 saved.")

## Plot 5: Donor-corrected â€” diagnosis + treatment -------------------------
p5 <- ggplot(pca_df_donor, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                               color = diagnosis, shape = treatment)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", na.value = "grey60") +
  labs(title    = "5.Plot â€” Donor-corrected (Biological variance)",
       subtitle = "Color = Diagnosis  |  Shape = Treatment",
       x = paste0("PC1: ", pctVar_donor[1], "% variance"),
       y = paste0("PC2: ", pctVar_donor[2], "% variance")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.caption = element_text(color = "firebrick", size = 8))

ggsave(file.path(plot_dir, "5.Plot_PCA_DonorCorrected_Diagnosis.png"),
       p5, width = 7, height = 6, dpi = 300)
message("Plot 5 saved.")

## Heatmap 4: Donor-corrected â€” treatment + diagnosis [Visualization only] -
make_heatmap(
  logcpm_matrix   = logcpm_no_donor,
  annotation_vars = c("treatment", "diagnosis"),
  title    = "4.Heatmap â€” Donor-corrected | Top 500 variable genes (Treatment + Diagnosis) [Visualization only]",
  out_file = file.path(plot_dir, "4.Heatmap_DonorCorrected_Treatment_Diagnosis.png")
)

```


## 9. Save corrected counts

```{r corrected counts}

combatseqcounts_df <- data.frame(Gene = rownames(combatseqcounts), combatseqcounts, check.names = FALSE)

write.csv(combatseqcounts_df, file=file.path(out_dir, "rawcounts_ComBatSeq.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```
